frame_analyses.description: FLS trainer overview showing all 5 tasks with colored equipment stations
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup phase - equipment visible but no active work yet

frame_analyses.description: First instrument visible entering the trainer - timing has begun
Both instruments now visible working with Penrose drain and suture material
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good bilateral instrument control, preparing for suture placement

frame_analyses.description: External view showing trainee preparing needle and suture outside trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper needle loading technique with 15cm 2-0 silk suture

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver and grasper positioning suture through first mark on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Accurate placement at first marking, good instrument coordination

frame_analyses.description: Suture being passed through both marks, creating working length for knot tying
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean passage through both marks with appropriate suture length

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot tying sequence - creating surgeon's knot (double throw)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand positioning for first throw with right hand dominance

frame_analyses.description: External view showing manual knot tying technique and hand positioning
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good finger dexterity and knot formation technique

frame_analyses.description: Using laparoscopic scissors to cut both suture ends inside trainer
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cutting technique with both ends trimmed appropriately

knot_assessments.notes: Good hand switching and single throw technique
Final throw with proper hand switching, knot appears secure

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears well-closed with no visible gaps after knot tightening

technique_summary: Excellent performance with efficient timing at 85 seconds. Proper surgeon's knot formation with correct hand switching between throws. Clean suture placement with minimal deviation.
improvement_suggestions: Consider slightly more precise initial suture placement to minimize deviation penalties
Work on maintaining consistent pace throughout all phases
strengths: Fast completion time showing good efficiency
Proper knot sequence with surgeon's knot and hand switching
Clean instrument handling and suture cutting technique
Good drain closure with no visible gaps
